search_queries_used: site:nice.org.uk ng118 renal ureteric stones imaging metabolic testing 2026
candidate_sources_rejected: analgesia, imaging, expulsive therapy, procedure and hydration advice
automatic diagnosis, investigation, treatment, medication, dose, procedure, referral, escalation, or discharge instructions
search snippets, landing pages, and broad source-family matches without an exact applicable section
rejection_reasons: Flank pain and urinary symptoms do not establish a stone.
No investigation, treatment, medicine, dose, procedure or advice is generated.
Autonomous clinical output is outside this documentation-only research programme and was not mapped.
Only opened exact official documents and directly applicable sections were accepted as evidence.
population_applicability: Adults, children or pregnant people with suspected or confirmed renal/ureteric stones; the imaging and management pathway is population-specific.
setting_applicability: Emergency and urology assessment; exact mappings are limited to already performed/reviewed results and clinician-entered follow-up.
UAE_applicability: Clinical concepts transfer, but UAE imaging, pregnancy, urology, laboratory, medicine and referral pathways govern.
recency_verification: Current NICE NG118 registered recommendations were reviewed on 2026-07-14.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact NICE stone-imaging section supports documenting existing test or imaging status only when performed or reviewed; it does not infer a stone or recommend imaging.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports clinician-reviewed CT or ultrasound imaging with age and pregnancy qualifiers.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact metabolic-testing section supports a clinician-entered documentation plan and arranged specialist or follow-up discussion only for a clinician-established stone context; it does not order tests.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports stone analysis, serum-calcium context, and qualified paediatric specialist metabolic assessment.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Recurrence-prevention recommendations were reviewed but not mapped to automatic advice.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports documenting hydration or dietary context and prevention advice only when discussed by the clinician; numeric instructions are not mapped.

unresolved_source_gaps: Pain history, urinary symptoms, fever, single kidney, pregnancy, examination, urinalysis, renal function, infection, diagnosis, treatment, medicine, dose, procedure and disposition remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
